2026-04-17T03:29:47.0600462Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T03:29:47.0601037Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T03:29:47.0601890Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T03:29:47.0602280Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T03:29:47.0602585Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:29:47.0602818Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:29:47.0602970Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-17T03:29:47.0603534Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T03:29:47.0604262Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T03:32:36.5777261Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T03:30:52.425043808Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58255,\"title\":\"[Conversations Authoring] Docs: Document new sub-client architecture in README and CHANGELOG\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58177) by `@jorgerangel-msft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58177 introduced four new resource-oriented sub-client classes as part of a major API refactoring of the Conversations Authoring SDK:\\n\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — project-level operations (create, delete, import, export, train, swap deployments, assign/unassign resources, etc.)\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deployment-level operations (deploy, get, delete, delete from resources, etc.)\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — trained model operations\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — exported model operations\\n\\nThe main `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client was significantly refactored (3,591 deletions, 16 additions), and collection result classes were renamed. All sample test files were updated to use the new sub-client pattern.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** — the `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` sections are all blank despite the introduction of four new sub-clients and a major refactoring of the primary client\\n- **README `Key concepts` section does not mention the new sub-clients** — the section only references `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` as the primary client; the new resource-oriented sub-client hierarchy (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`) is not described\\n- **README SDK version table is out of date** — the table only includes entries through `1.0.0-beta.3`; `1.0.0-beta.4` is missing along with its supported API versions\\n- **No samples exist for the new sub-clients** — all existing samples still use the flat `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` pattern; there are no samples demonstrating how to obtain and use the new sub-clients\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the existing `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` section to document the new sub-clients and breaking changes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added new resource-oriented sub-clients for more granular API access:\\n  - `ConversationAuthoringProject` — provides project-level operations including create, get, delete, import, export, train, cancel training, swap deployments, assign/unassign project resources, and resource status polling.\\n  - `ConversationAuthoringDeployment` — provides deployment-level operations including deploy project, get deployment, delete deployment, delete deployment from resources, and resource deletion status polling.\\n  - `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel` — provides trained model operations including get, delete, load snapshot, and model evaluation.\\n  - `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel` — provides exported model operations.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- The primary `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` client has been significantly refactored. Many operations previously available directly on `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` are now accessed via the new sub-clients (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`).\\n- Collection result classes for trained model evaluation results have been renamed for consistency.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md`\\n\\n**A) Update the SDK version table** (in the \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; section) to add the `1.0.0-beta.4` row. Locate this table:\\n\\n```markdown\\n|SDK version  |Supported API version of service\\n|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n|1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default)\\n|1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default)\\n|1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default)\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n|SDK version  |Supported API version of service\\n|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n|1.0.0-beta.1 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview (default)\\n|1.0.0-beta.2 | 2023-04-01, 2023-04-15-preview, 2024-11-15-preview, 2025-05-15-preview (default)\\n|1.0.0-beta.3 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default)\\n|1.0.0-beta.4 | 2023-04-01, 2025-11-01, 2025-05-15-preview, 2025-11-15-preview (default)\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Verify the exact supported API versions for `1.0.0-beta.4` by checking `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/src/Generated/ConversationAnalysisAuthoringClientOptions.cs` (or equivalent options class) before finalizing.\\n\\n**B) Update the `Key concepts` → `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client` section** to describe the sub-client architecture. Replace the existing paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client\\n\\nThe [ConversationAnalysisAuthoring][ConversationAnalysisAuthoring_class] is the top-level entry point for the Azure AI Conversation Authoring client library. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations and serves as the factory for the following resource-oriented sub-clients:\\n\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — create, get, delete, import, export, and train conversation projects; assign and unassign project resources; swap deployments.\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deploy projects, get and delete deployments, and manage deployment resource assignments.\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — get and delete trained models; load snapshots; retrieve evaluation summaries and results.\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — manage exported model snapshots.\\n\\nObtain sub-client instances from the primary client (check the generated client for the exact factory method names, e.g., `GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient()`).\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new sample test file demonstrating the new sub-client pattern:\\n\\n**Test file path:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample24_ConversationsAuthoring_SubClientArchitecture.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure;\\nusing Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class ConversationsAuthoringClientLiveTest : RecordedTestBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        public void Sample24_SubClientArchitecture()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_GetSubClients\\n            Uri endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;{endpoint}\\u0026#34;);\\n            AzureKeyCredential credential = new AzureKeyCredential(\\u0026#34;{api-key}\\u0026#34;);\\n            ConversationAnalysisAuthoring client = new ConversationAnalysisAuthoring(endpoint, credential);\\n\\n            // Obtain resource-oriented sub-clients from the primary client\\n            ConversationAuthoringProject projectClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient();\\n            ConversationAuthoringDeployment deploymentClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringDeploymentClient();\\n            ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel trainedModelClient = client.GetConversationAuthoringTrainedModelClient();\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Check the generated `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring.cs` for the exact factory method names for obtaining sub-clients before finalizing the snippet.\\n\\n**README location:** Insert a new entry in the `Examples` section listing the new sample, and add a snippet placeholder right after the `Key concepts` sub-client architecture description:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README has all required sections. The following items need updating:\\n\\n1. **Getting started → Install the package**: Update the SDK version table to include `1.0.0-beta.4` (see Step 1A above).\\n2. **Key concepts → ConversationAnalysisAuthoring Client**: Expand to describe the sub-client hierarchy (see Step 1B above).\\n3. **Examples**: Add a link to the new `Sample24` demonstrating how to obtain sub-clients.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24545958946/agentic_workflow) · ● 637.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58255\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T03:28:18Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T03:28:18Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

All sample test files were updated to use the new sub-client pattern.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.0.0-beta.4 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** — the `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` sections are all blank despite the introduction of four new sub-clients and a major refactoring of the primary client\\n- **README `Key concepts` section does not mention the new sub-clients** — the section only references `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` as the primary client; Many operations previously available directly on `ConversationAnalysisAuthoring` are now accessed via the new sub-clients (`ConversationAuthoringProject`, `ConversationAuthoringDeployment`, `ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`, `ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`).\\n- Collection result classes for trained model evaluation results have been renamed for consistency.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md`\\n\\n**A) Update the SDK version table** (in the \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; section) to add the `1.0.0-beta.4` row. It provides both synchronous and asynchronous operations and serves as the factory for the following resource-oriented sub-clients:\\n\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringProject`** — create, get, delete, import, export, and train conversation projects; assign and unassign project resources; swap deployments.\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringDeployment`** — deploy projects, get and delete deployments, and manage deployment resource assignments.\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringTrainedModel`** — get and delete trained models; load snapshots; retrieve evaluation summaries and results.\\n- **`ConversationAuthoringExportedModel`** — manage exported model snapshots.\\n\\nObtain sub-client instances from the primary client (check the generated client for the exact factory method names, e.g., `GetConversationAuthoringProjectClient()`).\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new sample test file demonstrating the new sub-client pattern:\\n\\n**Test file path:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample24_ConversationsAuthoring_SubClientArchitecture.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
